messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

The question should be why? While reverting gets us passing the test suite any real world user application using this function can fail to run. This is worrying in that newer core utils are moving to this function (newest cp uses this function).

Patch Set 1:

Note I push this patch to see if Lustre does work with copy_file_range(). The answer is NO!

Patch Set 2:

> The question should be why? While reverting gets us passing the test suite any real world user application using this function can fail to run. This is worrying in that newer core utils are moving to this function (newest cp uses this function).

lhsmtool_posix is a critical utility. It is not an ideal a place to test out new code. I do not think that it is using it correctly and your comments on other copy_file_range() changes indicate that it will actually degrade performance.

Patch Set 2:

Looks good to me.

commit.subject: LU-14208 utils: revert copy_file_range() usage
commit.message:
LU-14208 utils: revert copy_file_range() usage

Revert "LU-11621 utils: optimize lhsmtool_posix with
copy_file_range()". This is failing sanity-hsm test_55() on RHEL 8.3.

This reverts commit ccac422cf9dd3b2390d1c70cd42eff06d2f53be3.

Test-Parameters: clientdistro=el8.3 serverdistro=el8.3 testlist=sanity-hsm

Signed-off-by: John L. Hammond <jhammond@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: If622b8bf70778f35519a65f0de6418e50446e028
